'''''UFC 116: Lesnar vs. Carwin''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event held by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] on July 3, 2010, at the [[MGM Grand Garden Arena]] in [[Las Vegas, Nevada]].<ref name="ufc116">{{cite web |url=http://www.ufc.com/index.cfm?fa=EventDetail.home&eid=3262|title=UFC 116: LESNAR vs. CARWIN|publisher=ufc.com |date=May 4, 2010}}</ref> The show was voted as the Best Major Show in the 2010 [[List of Wrestling Observer Newsletter awards|Wrestling Observer Newsletter Awards]], the first and only time an [[mixed martial arts]] and UFC show won the award.

==Background==
[[Shane Carwin]], who won the [[UFC Heavyweight Championship|Interim Heavyweight Championship]] by defeating [[Frank Mir]] at [[UFC 111]], faced current heavyweight champion [[Brock Lesnar]]. The two were originally set to face each other at [[UFC 106]], then [[UFC 108]], before Carwin won the [[UFC Heavyweight Championship|interim heavyweight championship]], but Lesnar pulled out of the fight both times due to an illness ([[diverticulitis]]) that kept him from training.

[[Cheick Kongo]] was scheduled to face [[Roy Nelson (fighter)|Roy Nelson]],<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.mmaweekly.com/absolutenm/templates/dailynews.asp?articleid=11341&zoneid=13 |title=ROY NELSON VS CHEICK KONGO AT UFC 116- MMA WEEKLY – Mixed Martial Arts & UFC News, Photos, Rankings & more |publisher=Mma Weekly |access-date=May 16, 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100510233151/http://www.mmaweekly.com/absolutenm/templates/dailynews.asp?articleid=11341&zoneid=13 |archive-date=May 10, 2010 }}</ref> but a back injury sidelined Kongo.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://riddum.com/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=716:cheick-kongo-injured-no-confirmation-on-roy-nelson-matchup-nor-ufc-116-return&catid=50:ufc-news&Itemid=122 |title=Exclusive – Cheick Kongo: injured, no confirmation on Roy Nelson matchup nor UFC 116 return |publisher=Riddum.com |date=April 30, 2010 |access-date=May 16, 2010}}</ref> Nelson fought [[Junior dos Santos]] at [[UFC 117]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/19070/roy-nelson-vs-junior-dos-santos-in-the-works-for-ufc-117-main-card.mma |title=Roy Nelson vs. Junior Dos Santos in the works for UFC 117 main card |publisher=MMAjunkie.com |date=May 10, 2010 |access-date=May 16, 2010}}</ref>

[[Alessio Sakara]] was set to fight [[Nate Marquardt]],<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.mmaweekly.com/absolutenm/templates/dailynews.asp?articleid=11345&zoneid=13 |title=NATE MARQUARDT VS. ALESSIO SAKARA AT UFC 116- MMA WEEKLY – Mixed Martial Arts & UFC News, Photos, Rankings & more |publisher=Mma Weekly |access-date=May 16, 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100510232728/http://www.mmaweekly.com/absolutenm/templates/dailynews.asp?articleid=11345&zoneid=13 |archive-date=May 10, 2010 }}</ref> but Sakara pulled out of the bout after the death of his father.<ref>{{cite web|last=Holland |first=Jesse |url=http://www.mmamania.com/2010/5/2/1454804/report-alessio-sakara-withdraws |title=Report: Alessio Sakara withdraws from UFC 116 fight with Nate Marquardt |date=2 May 2010 |publisher=MMA Mania |access-date=May 16, 2010}}</ref>

[[Jacob Volkmann]] was scheduled to fight [[Paul Kelly (mixed martial arts fighter)|Paul Kelly]] as one of the preliminary fights, but Kelly reportedly had to pull out of the July 3 fight due to visa issues.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://twitter.com/JacobVolkmann/status/15221435412 |title=Twitter / Jacob Volkmann: I will not be fighting July 3rd in Vegas because the stupid brit did not get his visa in time. I will kill him Aug 3rd. |publisher=Twitter.com |access-date=June 13, 2010}}</ref> The bout was rescheduled for [[UFC Live: Jones vs. Matyushenko]] the next month.

Also, UFC newcomer [[Ricardo Romero (fighter)|Ricardo Romero]] was scheduled to fight [[Steve Cantwell]] after Cantwell's recent medical ban ended. However, the fight was cancelled for unknown reasons<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.heavy.com/mma/mma-news/2010/06/ricardo-romero-vs-steve-cantwell-in-the-works-for-ufc-116/|title=Ricardo Romero vs. Steve Cantwell In The Works For UFC 116|publisher=heavy.com |date=June 3, 2010}}</ref> and Cantwell was replaced by UFC returnee [[Seth Petruzelli]].<ref name="mmajunkie1">{{cite web |last=Marrocco |first=Steven |url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/19415/seth-petruzelli-rejoins-ufc-meets-ricardo-romero-on-ufc-116-preliminary-card.mma |title=Seth Petruzelli rejoins UFC, meets Ricardo Romero on UFC 116 preliminary card |publisher=MMAjunkie.com |date=June 4, 2010 |access-date=June 13, 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100608135647/http://mmajunkie.com/news/19415/seth-petruzelli-rejoins-ufc-meets-ricardo-romero-on-ufc-116-preliminary-card.mma |archive-date=June 8, 2010 }}</ref>

On June 17, 2010, Julio Paulino was injured and withdrew from his fight against [[Daniel Roberts (fighter)|Daniel Roberts]]. [[Forrest Petz]] returned to the UFC and replaced Paulino.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/19576/forrest-petz-replaces-injured-julio-paulino-meets-daniel-roberts-at-ufc-116.mma|title=Forrest Petz replaces injured Julio Paulino, meets Daniel Roberts at UFC 116|publisher=MMAjunkie|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100619051540/http://mmajunkie.com/news/19576/forrest-petz-replaces-injured-julio-paulino-meets-daniel-roberts-at-ufc-116.mma|archive-date=2010-06-19}}</ref>

On June 22, 2010, [[Wanderlei Silva]] had to withdraw from his co-main event fight against [[Yoshihiro Akiyama]] due to three broken ribs and an injured right knee. Akiyama was then matched against [[Chris Leben]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mmafighting.com/2010/06/22/wanderlei-silva-off-ufc-116|title=Wanderlei Silva Off UFC 116|author=Ariel Helwani|date=22 June 2010|publisher=MMA Fighting}}</ref>

The bout between [[Kendall Grove]] and [[Goran Reljic]] was originally slated for the live prelims show on Spike TV. However, it was later demoted and replaced by Seth Petruzelli vs. Ricardo Romero because of Grove's criticism of the Spike TV network and ''The Ultimate Fighter'' television show.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/19689/grove-vs-reljic-demoted-petruzelli-vs-romero-promoted-to-ufc-116s-ufc-prelims.mma|title=Grove vs. Reljic demoted, Petruzelli vs. Romero promoted to UFC 116's "UFC Prelims"|publisher=MMAjunkie|access-date=2010-06-25|archive-date=2010-06-27|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100627150339/http://mmajunkie.com/news/19689/grove-vs-reljic-demoted-petruzelli-vs-romero-promoted-to-ufc-116s-ufc-prelims.mma|url-status=dead}}</ref>

UFC President [[Dana White]] confirmed that the winner of the heavyweight title fight will defend the title against the undefeated [[Cain Velasquez]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/19769/ufcs-white-confirms-velasquez-fights-ufc-116s-lesnar-vs-carwin-winner.mma#comments|title=UFC's White confirms Velasquez fights UFC 116's Lesnar vs. Carwin winner|publisher=MMAjunkie|access-date=2010-07-01|archive-date=2012-05-25|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120525132821/http://mmajunkie.com/news/19769/ufcs-white-confirms-velasquez-fights-ufc-116s-lesnar-vs-carwin-winner.mma%23comments#comments|url-status=dead}}</ref>

UFC 116 won Best Major Show in the 2010 Wrestling Observer Newsletter awards.

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $75,000 bonuses.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2010/07/ufc-116-fighters-salaries-brock-lesnar-leads-xxx-payroll|title=UFC 116 fighters salaries: Brock Lesnar leads $923K payroll|publisher=MMAJunkie.com|date=July 6, 2010|access-date=July 6, 2010|author=Staff}}</ref>

*'''Fight of the Night: Krzysztof Soszynski vs. Stephan Bonnar''' and '''Yoshihiro Akiyama vs. Chris Leben'''
*'''Knockout of the Night: Gerald Harris'''
*'''Submission of the Night: Brock Lesnar'''

==Reported payout==
The following is the reported payout to the fighters as reported to the [[Nevada State Athletic Commission]]. It does not include sponsor money or "locker room" bonuses often given by the UFC and also do not include the UFC's traditional "fight night" bonuses.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/19820/ufc-116-fighters-salaries-brock-lesnar-leads-xxx-payroll.mma|title=UFC 116 fighters salaries: Brock Lesnar leads $923K payroll|publisher=mmajunkie.com|access-date=July 6, 2010|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100709053132/http://mmajunkie.com/news/19820/ufc-116-fighters-salaries-brock-lesnar-leads-xxx-payroll.mma|archive-date=July 9, 2010}}</ref>

*'''Brock Lesnar''' $400,000 (no win bonus) def. Shane Carwin ($40,000)
*'''Chris Leben''' $86,000 ($43,000 win bonus) def. Yoshihiro Akiyama ($45,000)
*'''Chris Lytle''' $52,000 ($26,000 win bonus) def. Matt Brown ($10,000)
*'''Stephan Bonnar''' $50,000 ($25,000 win bonus) def. Krzysztof Soszynski ($10,000)
*'''George Sotiropoulos''' $24,000 ($12,000 win bonus) def. Kurt Pellegrino ($25,000)
*'''Brendan Schaub''' $20,000 ($10,000 win bonus) def. Chris Tuchscherer ($12,000)
*'''Ricardo Romero''' $16,000 ($8,000 win bonus) def. Seth Petruzelli ($10,000)
*'''Kendall Grove''' $50,000 ($25,000 win bonus) def. Goran Reljic ($5,000)
*'''Gerald Harris''' $20,000 ($10,000 win bonus) def. Dave Branch($6,000)
*'''Daniel Roberts''' $12,000 ($6,000 win bonus) def. Forrest Petz ($6,000)
*'''Jon Madsen''' $16,000 ($8,000 win bonus) def. Karlos Vemola ($8,000)
